message:
Implement discovery summary display with priority breakdown (bd-73ys)

Add comprehensive discovery summary for ru review --dry-run:
- format_priority_badge(): Color-coded priority badges (CRITICAL/HIGH/NORMAL/LOW)
- show_discovery_summary_ansi(): ANSI terminal output with colors
- show_discovery_summary_gum(): Beautiful gum-styled output
- show_discovery_summary_json(): JSON output for automation
- show_discovery_summary(): Main function with mode detection

Summary shows:
- Total work items count
- Issues vs PRs breakdown
- Priority level distribution
- Top N items with truncated titles and colored badges

Closes: bd-73ys

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-704","title":"Implement url_to_clone_target()","description":"**Generate clone target for gh repo clone**

## What
Function to generate the owner/repo string that gh repo clone expects.

## Why
gh repo clone takes 'owner/repo', not full URL. We extract this from parsed URL.

## Implementation
```bash
url_to_clone_target() {
    local url="$1"
    local host owner repo
    parse_repo_url "$url" host owner repo || return 1
    echo "${owner}/${repo}"
}
```

## Note on Enterprise
For GitHub Enterprise, gh uses --hostname. Future enhancement.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Returns owner/repo format
- Works with all input URL formats","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:24:14.293471122-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:24:14.293471122-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all URL parsing functions: parse_repo_url, normalize_url, url_to_local_path, url_to_clone_target. All tests pass.","labels":["parsing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-704","depends_on_id":"bd-701","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.677356554-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"bd-705","title":"Implement sanitize_path_segment()","description":"**Sanitize path components**

## What
Remove or replace unsafe characters from path segments.

## Why
Malicious or unusual repo names could cause filesystem issues. Defense in depth.

## Implementation
```bash
sanitize_path_segment() {
    local segment="$1"
    # Remove leading/trailing whitespace
    segment="${segment#"${segment%%[![:space:]]*}"}"
    segment="${segment%"${segment##*[![:space:]]}"}"
    # Replace potentially problematic characters
    segment="${segment//\//_}"
    segment="${segment//\\/_}"
    segment="${segment//:/_}"
    echo "$segment"
}
```

## Characters to Handle
- Slashes (/ \)
- Colons (problematic on Windows)
- Leading dots (hidden files)
- Control characters

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dangerous characters replaced
- Empty segments rejected
- Normal names pass through unchanged","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:56:12.946839267-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:56:12.946839267-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented sanitize_path_segment() with comprehensive character replacement and safety checks","labels":["parsing","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-705","depends_on_id":"bd-7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.707274655-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"bd-706","title":"Create scripts/test_parsing.sh","description":"**Unit tests for URL parsing**

## What
Test script to verify URL parsing works for all formats.

## Why
Parsing is critical. Bugs here cause repos to be cloned to wrong locations or fail entirely.

## Test Cases
```bash
# HTTPS URLs
assert_parses "https://github.com/owner/repo" "github.com" "owner" "repo"
assert_parses "https://github.com/owner/repo.git" "github.com" "owner" "repo"

# SSH URLs
assert_parses "git@github.com:owner/repo.git" "github.com" "owner" "repo"

# Shorthand
assert_parses "owner/repo" "github.com" "owner" "repo"

# With host
assert_parses "github.com/owner/repo" "github.com" "owner" "repo"

# Normalization
assert_normalizes "git@github.com:owner/repo.git" "https://github.com/owner/repo"

# Local paths
assert_path "owner/repo" "flat" "/data/projects" "/data/projects/repo"
assert_path "owner/repo" "owner-repo" "/data/projects" "/data/projects/owner/repo"
assert_path "owner/repo" "full" "/data/projects" "/data/projects/github.com/owner/repo"
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All URL formats tested
- All layout modes tested
- Edge cases covered
- CI can run this script","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:23:24.700261824-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:23:24.700261824-05:00","close_reason":"URL parsing functions implemented and tested","labels":["parsing","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-706","depends_on_id":"bd-701","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.735921071-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-706","depends_on_id":"bd-702","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.766284111-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-706","depends_on_id":"bd-703","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.796279999-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-706","depends_on_id":"bd-704","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.824009347-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"bd-73ys","title":"Implement discovery summary display and formatting","description":"# Task: Implement Discovery Summary Display

## Purpose
Format and display discovered work items in a user-friendly summary, especially for --dry-run mode. This gives users visibility into what will be reviewed before sessions start.

## Background
After GraphQL discovery and priority scoring, users need to see:
- Total work items found across all repos
- Breakdown by priority level (CRITICAL/HIGH/NORMAL/LOW)
- Breakdown by type (issues vs PRs)
- Top N items that will be processed
- Repos with most activity

## Implementation

### show_discovery_summary()
```bash
show_discovery_summary() {
    local -n items_ref=$1
    local max_repos="${2:-$REVIEW_PARALLEL}"
    
    # Count totals
    local total=${#items_ref[@]}
    local critical=0 high=0 normal=0 low=0
    local issues=0 prs=0
    
    for item in "${items_ref[@]}"; do
        IFS="|" read -r repo type number title score level _ _ <<< "$item"
        case "$level" in
            CRITICAL) ((critical++)) ;;
            HIGH) ((high++)) ;;
            NORMAL) ((normal++)) ;;
            LOW) ((low++)) ;;
        esac
        case "$type" in
            issue) ((issues++)) ;;
            pr) ((prs++)) ;;
        esac
    done
    
    # Display summary
    if [[ "$GUM_AVAILABLE" == "true" ]]; then
        show_discovery_summary_gum
    else
        show_discovery_summary_ansi
    fi
}
```

### show_discovery_summary_gum()
```bash
show_discovery_summary_gum() {
    gum style --border rounded --padding "1 2" --border-foreground "#fab387" \
        "$(gum style --bold "Discovery Summary")"
    
    echo ""
    gum style "Total work items: $total"
    gum style "  Issues: $issues | PRs: $prs"
    echo ""
    
    # Priority breakdown with colors
    [[ $critical -gt 0 ]] && gum style --foreground "#f38ba8" "  CRITICAL: $critical"
    [[ $high -gt 0 ]] && gum style --foreground "#fab387" "  HIGH: $high"
    [[ $normal -gt 0 ]] && gum style --foreground "#f9e2af" "  NORMAL: $normal"
    [[ $low -gt 0 ]] && gum style --foreground "#6c7086" "  LOW: $low"
    echo ""
    
    # Top items preview
    gum style --bold "Top $max_repos items to review:"
    local i=0
    for item in "${items_ref[@]:0:$max_repos}"; do
        ((i++))
        IFS="|" read -r repo type number title score level _ _ <<< "$item"
        local badge
        badge=$(format_priority_badge "$level")
        echo "  $i. $badge $repo#$number: ${title:0:50}"
    done
}
```

### show_discovery_summary_ansi()
ANSI fallback version using escape codes:
```bash
show_discovery_summary_ansi() {
    local BOLD="\033[1m"
    local RED="\033[31m"
    local ORANGE="\033[33m"
    local YELLOW="\033[93m"
    local GRAY="\033[90m"
    local RESET="\033[0m"
    
    echo -e "${BOLD}Discovery Summary${RESET}"
    echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
    echo "Total work items: $total"
    echo "  Issues: $issues | PRs: $prs"
    echo ""
    echo "By priority:"
    [[ $critical -gt 0 ]] && echo -e "  ${RED}CRITICAL: $critical${RESET}"
    [[ $high -gt 0 ]] && echo -e "  ${ORANGE}HIGH: $high${RESET}"
    [[ $normal -gt 0 ]] && echo -e "  ${YELLOW}NORMAL: $normal${RESET}"
    [[ $low -gt 0 ]] && echo -e "  ${GRAY}LOW: $low${RESET}"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${BOLD}Top items to review:${RESET}"
    # ... similar item listing
}
```

### format_priority_badge()
```bash
format_priority_badge() {
    local level="$1"
    if [[ "$GUM_AVAILABLE" == "true" ]]; then
        case "$level" in
            CRITICAL) gum style --foreground "#f38ba8" --bold "CRITICAL" ;;
            HIGH) gum style --foreground "#fab387" "HIGH" ;;
            NORMAL) gum style --foreground "#f9e2af" "NORMAL" ;;
            LOW) gum style --foreground "#6c7086" "LOW" ;;
        esac
    else
        echo "[$level]"
    fi
}
```

### JSON Output Mode
```bash
show_discovery_summary_json() {
    jq -n \
        --argjson total "$total" \
        --argjson issues "$issues" \
        --argjson prs "$prs" \
        --argjson critical "$critical" \
        --argjson high "$high" \
        --argjson normal "$normal" \
        --argjson low "$low" \
        --argjson items "$(printf "%s\n" "${items_ref[@]}" | head -20 | jq -R -s "split(\"\n\") | map(select(. \!= \"\"))")" \
        "{
            total: \$total,
            by_type: {issues: \$issues, prs: \$prs},
            by_priority: {critical: \$critical, high: \$high, normal: \$normal, low: \$low},
            top_items: \$items
        }"
}
```

## Testing
- Verify gum formatting renders correctly
- Verify ANSI fallback works without gum
- Verify JSON output is valid
- Verify priority colors are correct
- Test with 0 items, 1 item, many items

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Summary displays item counts correctly
- [ ] Priority breakdown shows correct colors
- [ ] Top items listed with truncated titles
- [ ] Works with both gum and ANSI fallback
- [ ] JSON output mode for automation

 {"id":"bd-7itw","title":"Add review prerequisites to ru doctor command","description":"# Task: Add Review Prerequisites to Doctor

## Purpose
Extend `ru doctor` to check prerequisites for the review command, helping users diagnose why `ru review` might fail.

## Background
From AGENTS.md:
- doctor command does system diagnostics
- Should check for all dependencies

## New Doctor Checks for Review

### 1. Claude Code Installation
```bash
check_claude_code() {
    if \! command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
        log_error "Claude Code not installed"
        log_info "  Install: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check version for stream-json support
    local version
    version=$(claude --version 2>/dev/null | head -1)
    log_success "Claude Code installed: $version"
    return 0
}
```

### 2. Stream-JSON Support
```bash
check_stream_json_support() {
    # Test that stream-json format works
    if claude -p "test" --output-format stream-json --help 2>&1 | grep -q "unknown"; then
        log_error "Claude Code version does not support --output-format stream-json"
        log_info "  Update: npm update -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_success "Stream-JSON output format supported"
    return 0
}
```

### 3. tmux Installation (for local driver)
```bash
check_tmux() {
    if \! command -v tmux &>/dev/null; then
        log_warn "tmux not installed (required for local review driver)"
        log_info "  Install: brew install tmux  OR  apt install tmux"
        return 1
    fi
    
    local version
    version=$(tmux -V 2>/dev/null)
    log_success "tmux installed: $version"
    return 0
}
```

### 4. ntm Installation (optional, for advanced driver)
```bash
check_ntm() {
    if \! command -v ntm &>/dev/null; then
        log_info "ntm not installed (optional, for advanced review mode)"
        log_info "  See: https://github.com/dicklesworthstone/ntm"
        return 0  # Not an error, just informational
    fi
    
    # Check robot mode support
    if \! ntm --help 2>&1 | grep -q "robot"; then
        log_warn "ntm installed but robot mode not available"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_success "ntm installed with robot mode support"
    return 0
}
```

### 5. jq Installation
```bash
check_jq() {
    if \! command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
        log_error "jq not installed (required for review command)"
        log_info "  Install: brew install jq  OR  apt install jq"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_success "jq installed"
    return 0
}
```

### 6. GitHub API Rate Limit
```bash
check_github_rate_limit() {
    local remaining reset
    remaining=$(gh api rate_limit --jq ".resources.core.remaining" 2>/dev/null)
    reset=$(gh api rate_limit --jq ".resources.core.reset" 2>/dev/null)
    
    if [[ -z "$remaining" ]]; then
        log_warn "Could not check GitHub rate limit"
        return 1
    fi
    
    if [[ $remaining -lt 100 ]]; then
        local reset_time
        reset_time=$(date -d "@$reset" 2>/dev/null || date -r "$reset" 2>/dev/null)
        log_warn "GitHub API rate limit low: $remaining remaining"
        log_info "  Resets at: $reset_time"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_success "GitHub API rate limit OK: $remaining remaining"
    return 0
}
```

### 7. Review State Directory
```bash
check_review_state_dir() {
    local state_dir="${RU_STATE_DIR:-$HOME/.local/state/ru}"
    
    if [[ \! -d "$state_dir" ]]; then
        log_info "Review state directory will be created on first run"
        return 0
    fi
    
    if [[ \! -w "$state_dir" ]]; then
        log_error "Review state directory not writable: $state_dir"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_success "Review state directory OK: $state_dir"
    return 0
}
```

## Integration

### Doctor Output Section
```
Review Prerequisites:
  ✓ Claude Code installed: 1.0.0
  ✓ Stream-JSON output format supported
  ✓ tmux installed: tmux 3.3a
  ℹ ntm not installed (optional)
  ✓ jq installed
  ✓ GitHub API rate limit OK: 4850 remaining
  ✓ Review state directory OK
```

### Conditional Checks
Only run review checks if:
- User runs `ru doctor --review`
- User has ever run `ru review` before
- There is a review config file

## Testing
- Verify each check works independently
- Verify missing dependencies detected
- Verify output formatting correct
- Verify --review flag works

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Claude Code check works
- [ ] Stream-JSON support check works
- [ ] tmux/ntm checks work
- [ ] jq check works
- [ ] Rate limit check works
- [ ] State directory check works
- [ ] Clean doctor output format

 {"id":"bd-7of4","title":"Implement drill-down view for detailed question context","description":"Drill-down view shows full context for a single question.

Layout:
+---------------------------------------------------------------+
| project-alpha - Session Detail                           [ESC] |
+---------------------------------------------------------------+
| Repository: https://github.com/owner/project-alpha             |
| Session ID: ru-review-project-alpha                            |
| Duration: 12m 34s | Context: 45%                               |
+---------------------------------------------------------------+
| ISSUE #42: Authentication failing on Windows                   |
+---------------------------------------------------------------+
| Reported by: @user123 on 2024-12-15 (20 days ago)             |
| Description: When I try to login on Windows 11...              |
+---------------------------------------------------------------+
| CLAUDE ANALYSIS                                                |
| I verified this issue. Root cause in auth.py:234:              |
|   config_path = home_dir + "/config/auth.json"                 |
| Options:                                                        |
|   A: Minimal fix (5 lines, low risk)                           |
|   B: Full refactor (45 lines, medium risk)                     |
+---------------------------------------------------------------+
| [a] Quick fix  [b] Full refactor  [c] Skip  [v] View session   |
+---------------------------------------------------------------+

open_drilldown() renders full view, handles a/b/c quick answers, v shows raw session output, Esc returns to dashboard.

+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# DISCOVERY SUMMARY DISPLAY (bd-73ys)
+# Format and display discovered work items in a user-friendly summary
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+# Format a priority level with color (ANSI or plain)
+# Args: level (CRITICAL/HIGH/NORMAL/LOW)
+# Output: Formatted string
+format_priority_badge() {
+    local level="$1"
+    local use_color="${2:-true}"
+
+    if [[ "$use_color" != "true" ]]; then
+        echo "[$level]"
+        return
+    fi
+
+    # ANSI color codes
+    local RED="\033[31m"
+    local ORANGE="\033[33m"
+    local YELLOW="\033[93m"
+    local GRAY="\033[90m"
+    local RESET="\033[0m"
+    local BOLD="\033[1m"
+
+    case "$level" in
+        CRITICAL) echo -e "${BOLD}${RED}[CRITICAL]${RESET}" ;;
+        HIGH)     echo -e "${ORANGE}[HIGH]${RESET}" ;;
+        NORMAL)   echo -e "${YELLOW}[NORMAL]${RESET}" ;;
+        LOW)      echo -e "${GRAY}[LOW]${RESET}" ;;
+        *)        echo "[$level]" ;;
+    esac
+}
+
+# Show discovery summary using ANSI formatting
+# Args: total issues prs critical high normal low max_display items_array_ref
+show_discovery_summary_ansi() {
+    local total="$1" issues="$2" prs="$3"
+    local critical="$4" high="$5" normal="$6" low="$7"
+    local max_display="$8"
+    local -n _items_ansi=$9
+
+    local BOLD="\033[1m"
+    local RED="\033[31m"
+    local ORANGE="\033[33m"
+    local YELLOW="\033[93m"
+    local GRAY="\033[90m"
+    local CYAN="\033[36m"
+    local RESET="\033[0m"
+
+    echo "" >&2
+    echo -e "${BOLD}━━━ Discovery Summary ━━━${RESET}" >&2
+    echo "" >&2
+    echo -e "Total work items: ${BOLD}$total${RESET}" >&2
+    echo -e "  Issues: ${CYAN}$issues${RESET} | PRs: ${CYAN}$prs${RESET}" >&2
+    echo "" >&2
+    echo -e "${BOLD}By priority:${RESET}" >&2
+    [[ $critical -gt 0 ]] && echo -e "  ${RED}CRITICAL: $critical${RESET}" >&2
+    [[ $high -gt 0 ]] && echo -e "  ${ORANGE}HIGH: $high${RESET}" >&2
+    [[ $normal -gt 0 ]] && echo -e "  ${YELLOW}NORMAL: $normal${RESET}" >&2
+    [[ $low -gt 0 ]] && echo -e "  ${GRAY}LOW: $low${RESET}" >&2
+    echo "" >&2
+
+    if [[ ${#_items_ansi[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
+        local display_count=$max_display
+        [[ $display_count -gt ${#_items_ansi[@]} ]] && display_count=${#_items_ansi[@]}
+
+        echo -e "${BOLD}Top $display_count items to review:${RESET}" >&2
+        local i=0
+        for item in "${_items_ansi[@]:0:$display_count}"; do
+            ((i++))
+            IFS="|" read -r repo_id item_type number title labels created_at updated_at is_draft <<< "$item"
+
+            # Calculate score and level for display
+            local score level
+            score=$(calculate_item_priority_score "$item_type" "$labels" "$created_at" "$updated_at" "$is_draft" "$repo_id" "$number")
+            level=$(get_priority_level "$score")
+            local badge
+            badge=$(format_priority_badge "$level" "true")
+
+            # Truncate title if too long
+            local short_title="${title:0:45}"
+            [[ ${#title} -gt 45 ]] && short_title="${short_title}..."
+
+            echo -e "  $i. $badge ${CYAN}${repo_id}${RESET}#${number}: $short_title" >&2
+        done
+        echo "" >&2
+    fi
+}
+
+# Show discovery summary using gum (if available)
+# Args: total issues prs critical high normal low max_display items_array_ref
+show_discovery_summary_gum() {
+    local total="$1" issues="$2" prs="$3"
+    local critical="$4" high="$5" normal="$6" low="$7"
+    local max_display="$8"
+    local -n _items_gum=$9
+
+    # Header
+    gum style --border rounded --padding "0 2" --border-foreground "#fab387" \
+        "$(gum style --bold 'Discovery Summary')" >&2
+
+    echo "" >&2
+    gum style "Total work items: $total" >&2
+    gum style "  Issues: $issues | PRs: $prs" >&2
+    echo "" >&2
+
+    # Priority breakdown with colors
+    gum style --bold "By priority:" >&2
+    [[ $critical -gt 0 ]] && gum style --foreground "#f38ba8" "  CRITICAL: $critical" >&2
+    [[ $high -gt 0 ]] && gum style --foreground "#fab387" "  HIGH: $high" >&2
+    [[ $normal -gt 0 ]] && gum style --foreground "#f9e2af" "  NORMAL: $normal" >&2
+    [[ $low -gt 0 ]] && gum style --foreground "#6c7086" "  LOW: $low" >&2
+    echo "" >&2
+
+    if [[ ${#_items_gum[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
+        local display_count=$max_display
+        [[ $display_count -gt ${#_items_gum[@]} ]] && display_count=${#_items_gum[@]}
+
+        gum style --bold "Top $display_count items to review:" >&2
+        local i=0
+        for item in "${_items_gum[@]:0:$display_count}"; do
+            ((i++))
+            IFS="|" read -r repo_id item_type number title labels created_at updated_at is_draft <<< "$item"
+
+            local score level badge_color
+            score=$(calculate_item_priority_score "$item_type" "$labels" "$created_at" "$updated_at" "$is_draft" "$repo_id" "$number")
+            level=$(get_priority_level "$score")
+
+            case "$level" in
+                CRITICAL) badge_color="#f38ba8" ;;
+                HIGH) badge_color="#fab387" ;;
+                NORMAL) badge_color="#f9e2af" ;;
+                *) badge_color="#6c7086" ;;
+            esac
+
+            local short_title="${title:0:45}"
+            [[ ${#title} -gt 45 ]] && short_title="${short_title}..."
+
+            echo -n "  $i. " >&2
+            gum style --foreground "$badge_color" --inline "[$level]" >&2
+            echo " ${repo_id}#${number}: $short_title" >&2
+        done
+        echo "" >&2
+    fi
+}
+
+# Show discovery summary as JSON (for automation)
+# Args: total issues prs critical high normal low max_display items_array_ref
+show_discovery_summary_json() {
+    local total="$1" issues="$2" prs="$3"
+    local critical="$4" high="$5" normal="$6" low="$7"
+    local max_display="$8"
+    local -n _items_json=$9
+
+    local items_json="[]"
+
+    if [[ ${#_items_json[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
+        local display_count=$max_display
+        [[ $display_count -gt ${#_items_json[@]} ]] && display_count=${#_items_json[@]}
+
+        local item_list=""
+        for item in "${_items_json[@]:0:$display_count}"; do
+            IFS="|" read -r repo_id item_type number title labels created_at updated_at is_draft <<< "$item"
+
+            local score level
+            score=$(calculate_item_priority_score "$item_type" "$labels" "$created_at" "$updated_at" "$is_draft" "$repo_id" "$number")
+            level=$(get_priority_level "$score")
+
+            # Escape title for JSON
+            local escaped_title
+            escaped_title=$(echo "$title" | jq -Rs '.')
+
+            [[ -n "$item_list" ]] && item_list+=","
+            item_list+="{\"repo\":\"$repo_id\",\"type\":\"$item_type\",\"number\":$number,\"title\":$escaped_title,\"score\":$score,\"level\":\"$level\"}"
+        done
+        items_json="[$item_list]"
+    fi
+
+    cat <<EOF
+{
+  "total": $total,
+  "by_type": {"issues": $issues, "prs": $prs},
+  "by_priority": {"critical": $critical, "high": $high, "normal": $normal, "low": $low},
+  "top_items": $items_json
+}
+EOF
+}
+
+# Main discovery summary function
+# Args: work_items (pipe-separated strings)
 show_discovery_summary() {
     local items=("$@")
+    local max_display="${REVIEW_PARALLEL:-5}"
 
     if [[ ${#items[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
         log_info "No work items to review"
         return
     fi

+

+
+        # Calculate score and count by priority level

+        level=$(get_priority_level "$score")
+
+        case "$level" in

+            NORMAL) ((normal++)) ;;
+            LOW) ((low++)) ;;

+    done
+
+    # Display based on output mode

+    elif [[ "$GUM_AVAILABLE" == "true" ]] && [[ -t 2 ]]; then

+    fi
